title: Reporting Local Problems
summary: Guidance for reporting drainage issues, roads, waste complaints, or other neighbourhood concerns.
body: Guidance for addressing drainage issues, roads, waste complaints, and neighbourhood concerns through the proper local channels.
steps: Document the exact location, nature of the issue, the date of observation, and any safety risks to residents.
Contact the NDC, Municipality, or relevant local office first, and record the officer or department details.
For urgent matters, follow up by telephone and request guidance on subsequent actions.
office: NDC, Municipality, or relevant local office

title: NDC Services
summary: Common services citizens can request through their Neighbourhood Democratic Council, including property compliance, complaints, drains, burial spot guidance and local certificates.
body: Neighbourhood Democratic Councils provide front-line local services for residents, property owners and community groups. This page summarises the most common NDC requests shared for the Ministry review and guides citizens to the right next step.
steps: Find the NDC responsible for your property, neighbourhood or community using the council directory.

Choose the service you need: property compliance, complaint handling, drainage maintenance, burial spot guidance, house plan stamping, permission for family fun day activities, food handler certificate renewal, cash grant registration or Single Window guidance.
Prepare your identification, property details, approval documents or supporting information before visiting or submitting a request.
If the matter is a complaint or urgent community issue, use the online helpdesk so it can be logged and tracked.
documents: Property address, lot number or transport details where relevant
Approved house plan copies for NDC stamping where relevant
Any prior receipt, certificate, approval letter or reference number
Photos or exact location details for complaints, drains or maintenance matters

contact.address: Contact your local NDC office or the Ministry, Fort Street, Georgetown, Guyana

title: Rates and Taxes Guidance
summary: Guidance for property owners who need to understand where to pay or update local tax records.
body: Guidance for property owners who need to understand where to pay or update local tax records.
steps: Contact the local authority responsible for the property location to confirm the current account balance and payment process.
Carry the property address, transport or lot number and any previous receipt if available.
Ask whether payment is handled at the council office, treasury section or another designated point.
office: Local authority responsible for the property location (council office, treasury section, or another designated payment point)

title: Community Projects
summary: Guidance for community groups seeking support for local clean-up, beautification, or development activities.
body: Guidance for community groups seeking support for local clean-up, beautification, or development activities.
steps: Document the community problem, proposed activity, expected benefit, and location.
Contact the NDC, Municipality, or RDC office to determine whether submission goes through councillors or the administrative office.
Record the contact person's name and any reference number for easier follow-up.

title: Business Licences
summary: Guidance for opening or regularising a business that operates within a municipality or NDC area.
body: Guidance for opening or regularising a business that operates within a municipality or NDC area.
steps: Check with the local authority where the business will operate to confirm licensing, trade and sanitation requirements.
Carry identification, business name details, address information and any tenancy or ownership documents.
Ask whether other agencies must also approve the activity before the local licence can be finalised.
office: Local authority in the Municipality or NDC area where the business will operate

title: Building Permits
summary: Guidance for persons planning new construction, extensions, renovations, or structural changes.
body: Guidance for persons planning new construction, extensions, renovations, or structural changes.
steps: Visit your NDC, Municipality or local authority first to confirm whether approval is needed for the proposed works.
Prepare a simple description of the project, proof of ownership or permission to build, and any plan or sketch available.
Ask what fees, inspections, and supporting documents are required before work begins.
documents: Proof of ownership or permission to build
office: NDC, Municipality, or local authority in your area

title: Vendor and Supplier Enquiries
summary: Guidance for businesses seeking information about procurement opportunities and vendor registration.
body: Guidance to companies interested in engaging with the Ministry's procurement processes and becoming registered suppliers.
steps: Prepare your business registration details, tax compliance information, and contact details.
Check tender notices and ask the Ministry or procuring entity what supporting documents are required for registration or bidding.
Keep copies of submissions and note all deadlines carefully.
documents: Supporting documents as specified by the procuring entity
office: Ministry of Local Government & Regional Development
